name: What's Your Communication Love Language?
description: Discover how you prefer to give and receive emotional communication

verbal-affirmer.title: Verbal Affirmer
verbal-affirmer.description: You thrive on words of affirmation and express yourself through language. Heartfelt compliments, encouraging words, and verbal appreciation mean the world to you in relationships.
verbal-affirmer.challenges: May sometimes over-rely on verbal validation

quality-timer.title: Quality Timer
quality-timer.description: You value undivided attention and express love through presence. For you, meaningful connection happens when you're fully engaged with someone without distractions.
quality-timer.strengths: Present, attentive, good listener, creates meaningful moments
quality-timer.challenges: May feel hurt by distractions during conversations

gift-giver.title: Gift Giver
gift-giver.description: You notice details and express affection through thoughtful offerings. For you, carefully chosen gifts symbolize care and remembrance in relationships.
gift-giver.challenges: May sometimes equate gift value with relationship value

service-provider.title: Service Provider
service-provider.description: You show love through practical support and helpful actions. For you, actions truly speak louder than words when it comes to expressing care.
service-provider.challenges: May sometimes overlook verbal expressions of affection

questions.question: You feel most loved when someone:
options: Tells you how much they appreciate you
Spends quality time with just you
Gives you a meaningful gift
Does something helpful for you

questions.question: Your preferred way to show affection is:
options: Telling them how much they mean to you
Spending one-on-one time together
Giving them thoughtful presents
Doing practical things to help them

questions.question: In relationships, you're most likely to:
options: Write heartfelt notes or texts
Plan special activities just for two
Remember important dates with gifts
Show up to help when needed

questions.question: You feel hurt when:
options: Someone doesn't acknowledge your efforts verbally
Your partner is distracted during your time together
Special occasions pass without recognition
People don't follow through on promises to help

questions.question: Your ideal date would involve:
options: Deep conversation over coffee
An activity where you focus just on each other
Exchanging meaningful gifts
Working on a project together

questions.question: You most appreciate when friends:
options: Tell you how much they value you
Give you their full attention when together
Remember your birthday with a special gift
Help you move or with other big tasks

questions.question: Your love language is most about:
options: Hearing words of affirmation
Receiving undivided attention
Getting thoughtful presents
Seeing helpful actions
